entries.rule: Surface.IsoCurve(0, c) varies the first parameter and keeps the second constant. Surface.IsoCurve(1, c) keeps the first parameter constant and varies the second.
entries.implication: Do not label U-lines and V-lines from memory. Restate which parameter is constant and which varies before writing code.

entries.rule: BrepFace.TrimAwareIsoCurve(direction, constantParameter) may return multiple curves.
entries.implication: Expect Curve[] and preserve grouping when useful. A flat list can lose logical structure.

entries.rule: A Surface does not preserve Brep face trim context. If the visible boundary matters, prefer BrepFace over Surface.
entries.implication: Using Surface or UnderlyingSurface() can produce geometry outside the visible trimmed face.
entries.source_ref: RhinoCommon BrepFace docs and McNeel forum discussions about trimmed vs underlying surfaces

entries.rule: Curve.DivideByCount(count, includeEnds) returns curve parameters on success, not Point3d values, unless using the overload with an out Point3d array.
entries.implication: Convert returned parameters with curve.PointAt(t) or call the overload that fills points explicitly.
entries.source_ref: Rhino 8 System/RhinoCommon.xml members M:Rhino.Geometry.Curve.DivideByCount(System.Int32,System.Boolean) and overload with Point3d[]

entries.rule: BrepFace.OrientationIsReversed indicates that the face orientation is opposite the natural surface orientation.
entries.implication: When the task depends on normals, frames, or sidedness, account for face orientation instead of assuming natural surface orientation.

entries.rule: Trim-aware queries near face domain boundaries can fail or behave unexpectedly when the chosen parameter lies outside the active trimmed region.
entries.implication: Do not assume face Domain() endpoints are always safe trim-aware sampling values. Interior sampling or a small epsilon may be needed.
entries.source_ref: McNeel forum thread on BrepFace.TrimAwareIsoCurve and domain boundaries

entries.rule: In Grasshopper scripting, Single Item access means the component can execute once per item when fed a list, not once for the whole list.
entries.implication: Choose input access deliberately and avoid accidental per-item execution when the algorithm expects the full collection.
entries.source_ref: Grasshopper C# essentials and scripting docs on RunScript execution and parameter access

entries.rule: Grasshopper script outputs are object-typed and rely on conversion after script execution.
entries.implication: Initialize outputs early and ensure final assigned values match what Grasshopper can convert cleanly.
entries.source_ref: Grasshopper scripting docs section Outputs Are Objects

entries.rule: Script input parameters are optional by default in Rhino 8 Grasshopper scripting.
entries.implication: Generated nodes should usually remain neutral on empty inputs unless the task explicitly requires mandatory data.
entries.source_ref: Grasshopper scripting docs section Required Inputs
